name: study-5-adversarial-probes
title: Adversarial probes — can the framework be fooled?
objective:
Turn the demonstration into a test: challenge the autopoiesis framework with
systems that should NOT qualify, and check that the metric REJECTS them. Two
probes — an externally-maintained mimic (persistence supplied from outside, not
self-produced) and a network missing a self-production step (no membrane
producer). The study passes only if the metric rejects both, showing the
criteria discriminate genuine self-production from superficial similarity.

pipeline_gate.proceed_condition:
The framework rejects both adversarial probes — the externally-maintained
mimic fails precariousness and the incomplete network fails operational
closure — so the criteria have demonstrated discriminative power.

behavior_tests.name: rejects-external-maintenance
behavior_tests.description:
An externally-maintained mimic (membrane clamped from outside) persists when
starved; the metric must flag it as NOT precarious, i.e. reject it.

provenance.note: The mimic must be ≥3x more persistent than the self-producing loop when starved to count as "rejected" (not precarious); calibrated against the self-producing-loop positive control, mirroring study-1's precariousness band.

behavior_tests.name: rejects-broken-closure
behavior_tests.description:
A network missing the membrane producer cannot self-produce its boundary;
operational closure must leave a non-empty gap, i.e. reject it.

provenance.note: Any non-empty closure gap means a required type is not self-produced, so the network is definitionally not operationally closed — the threshold is the autopoietic closure definition, not a tuned value.

falsifiability:
The framework would FAIL this adversarial test if the externally-maintained
mimic collapsed like a self-producing loop (precariousness indistinguishable
from external upkeep), or if the membrane-less network achieved a closed gap —
i.e. if a superficially-similar or externally-sustained system passed.

deficit_addressed.note: Calibrates the operational-closure + precariousness criteria by confronting them with systems that should NOT qualify (an externally-maintained mimic and a network missing its membrane producer). closure_gap_item is auto-filled with the gap the membrane producer closes in the broken probe.

alternative_hypotheses.claim: Passing the four constructive studies just shows the criteria were tuned to those models, not that they discriminate.
alternative_hypotheses.discriminated_by: the adversarial probes are constructed to satisfy surface features yet are rejected, so the criteria track organization, not the specific models.

resolved_uncertainties: The precariousness and closure criteria reject externally-maintained and structurally-incomplete systems — they have discriminative power, not just confirmatory fit.
remaining_uncertainties: Only two adversarial archetypes are probed; parasitic / partially-dependent and random-cyclic networks remain to be tested.

alternate_hypotheses.hypothesis: A cleverly-tuned random cyclic network might still score a closed gap.
alternate_hypotheses.why_plausible: closure is a structural bookkeeping check; a hand-crafted cycle could satisfy it without biological meaning.
discriminating_observables: operational_closure gap on a randomly-wired network
precariousness of a parasitic agent that exploits a host

followup_study_proposals.title: Parasitic / partially-dependent system
followup_study_proposals.motivation: Blur self-production vs dependence (parasites, endosymbionts, reduced genomes) and check where the criteria draw the line.

followup_study_proposals.title: Random cyclic network
followup_study_proposals.motivation: Test whether a meaningless cyclic network can be made to score a closed gap (would expose closure as mere bookkeeping).

findings.statement: The framework REJECTS an externally-maintained mimic: clamped from outside it persists when starved (precariousness fails), so the metric does not mistake external upkeep for self-production.

The framework REJECTS a network missing self-production: removing the membrane producer leaves a non-empty closure gap (['membrane_lipids']), so a structurally-incomplete network does not pass operational closure.

robustness.note: 5/5: the externally-maintained mimic stays non-precarious; the broken-network gap is structural.

controls.name: self-producing-loop
controls.hypothesis: The genuine self-producing loop should be ACCEPTED (closed + precarious).

controls.name: externally-maintained-mimic
controls.hypothesis: A system maintained from OUTSIDE mimics persistence but is not self-producing — the metric must REJECT it (precariousness fails).
controls.observed: 9x more persistent than the self-producing loop when starved

controls.name: missing-self-production-network
controls.hypothesis: A network missing a self-production step (no membrane producer) should FAIL operational closure.
